title: Dioclea multiflora
intro: Dioclea multiflora, also known as the "Makabuhay" plant, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family (Fabaceae), native to the Philippines and other parts of Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, with health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antidiabetic, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Dioclea multiflora include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Dioclea multiflora is commonly prepared as a tea or decoction, and is also used to make capsules and tinctures, which are used to treat conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Dioclea multiflora is a species of plant in the family Fabaceae. It belongs to the genus Dioclea, which comprises around 40 species of flowering plants native to tropical Asia and Australia. Dioclea multiflora is further classified as a member of the tribe Diocleae, within the subfamily Faboideae. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its compound leaves and pea-like flowers. Further study may be needed to confirm the accuracy of this classification.
uses: Dioclea multiflora is known to have various medicinal uses, including being used to treat fever and other related conditions. The plant's extracts have been found to possess antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent the growth of bacteria and fungi. It is also used in traditional medicine to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and stomach problems. Furthermore, the plant has been reported to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate pain and swelling. Traditional healers have been using Dioclea multiflora for centuries to treat various ailments, showcasing its potential as a natural remedy.

Some research suggests that it may have anti-inflammatory properties, which could help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis.
